Patch Set 8:

> This evaluates to:
> tar -czvf /tmp/lutf.hostname.tar.gz -C / /tmp/lutf

> I don't think you can just replace the space with a '.'.

"." is "tar entire current dir which is what you want. the "current" dir is the one you specify with -C

We definitely do not want -C / what we want is 
-C /tmp/lutf or some such, that way we won't have tmp/lutf as two extra directories in all such archives?

Patch Set 10:

I'm not sure what you're looking for.

The tar you pasted below is not what I generate. That's what's generated by your collection script. The code I changed generates the tar on each of the agents and scps it over to the master node. It places it in /tmp/lutf/ (in the example you showed)

IE:

tmp/lutf/lutf.oleg259-server.tar.gz

What exactly is not working for you?

parents.subject: LU-13621 lnet: utility to print cpt number

commit.subject: LU-10973 lutf: use configured tmp directory for tar
commit.message:
LU-10973 lutf: use configured tmp directory for tar

After the LUTF run is done all the test results on all
the agent nodes need to be tarred, in order to make them
available for review later on. Don't assume the lutf tmp
files are in /tmp/lutf. Use the tmp-dir directory configured
in the lutf configuration.

Add the master only once to the agent list.

Pass PYTHOPATH to agent.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Amir Shehata <ashehata@whamcloud.com>
